title: Bidirectional visual canvas: code-to-diagram projection with live overlay
purpose: Reframes RM-009 ('declarative DSL + visual builder') away from a separate DSL and toward a generated, always-in-sync canvas (RM-009 as designed here). Because a workflow is a typed function over a small known vocabulary (run / spawn / receive / all / race / sleep), the diagram can be generated from the source, overlaid with a live run, and edited within bounds — a projection of the typed source, never a second authoring surface (ADR-014).
task: Make the diagram a faithful projection of the typed source. In: extract a workflow's primitive structure from the package and render it to a graph automatically; overlay a live run's progress on the same graph; and regenerate Gleam from bounded structural edits such that it still type-checks. Out: unbounded diagram-to-code synthesis, and any path that makes the canvas the authoritative artifact.

requirements.title: Structure extraction and automatic graph rendering
requirements.spec: WHEN rendering a workflow, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract its primitive structure (run / spawn / receive / all / race / sleep and control flow) from the package and render a graph automatically. The rendered node and edge set SHALL match the workflow's actual structure.
acceptance: Structure extraction yields the node and edge set for a workflow from the package.
Rendering examples/order-saga produces a graph whose node/edge set matches the workflow's known structure, proven by a diff test (C23).
The diagram is generated from the source, not hand-drawn.

requirements.title: Live execution overlay and bounded round-trip
requirements.spec: WHILE a run executes, THE SYSTEM SHALL overlay its progress on the same graph, lighting the active node as the engine emits events. WHEN a bounded structural edit is made on the canvas, THE SYSTEM SHALL regenerate Gleam that still type-checks. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT make the canvas the authoritative artifact.
acceptance: A live run lights the active node on the graph as events arrive over the existing event stream.
A bounded structural edit on the canvas regenerates Gleam that compiles under gleam build (C24).
The canvas is a projection — the typed source remains the source of truth (CN6).

boundaries: SHALL NOT support unbounded diagram-to-code synthesis — only bounded structural round-trip that regenerates type-checking Gleam.
SHALL NOT make the canvas the authoritative artifact; the typed source is the source of truth (ADR-014).
SHALL NOT change the event stream or the package format.
verification: Node/edge diff test against a known workflow structure.
Regenerated Gleam from a bounded edit type-checks.
